closure_requirements: Persist audit_stream_ref + stream_sequence ordering.
Retain object refs, reason codes, hashes, and lineage when payloads expire.
notes: Audit exists to prove what happened, not to optimize runtime tuning.
rows.retention_boundary: Durable append-only retention with explicit payload_availability_state and audit_sufficiency_state when payload bodies age out.
rows.retry_or_resolution_posture: Not retried as telemetry; proves material decisions and legal state transitions.
rows.source_heading_or_logical_block: 14.2 Separation of concerns / A. Audit evidence and 14.5 Audit event contract
rows.visibility_boundary: Append-only audit and investigation surfaces; customer-safe derivatives require explicit masking or separate projections.

closure_requirements: Error spans require typed failure_class and failure_phase detail.
Sampling may not remove mandatory audit anchors.
notes: Trace span taxonomy freezes 22 minimum span codes.
Sampling posture enumerates MANDATORY_FORENSIC, DETERMINISTIC_RETAIN, SAMPLED_OPERATIONAL.
rows.producer_or_owner: Run-engine orchestration and service runtime instrumentation
rows.retention_boundary: Potentially sampled, except filing-, retention-, authority-, and replay-critical spans which stay deterministic-retain or mandatory-forensic.
rows.retry_or_resolution_posture: Typed error spans drive remediation only when correlated ErrorRecord lineage exists.
rows.visibility_boundary: Operational investigation surfaces and run-timeline views; not the proof of record.

closure_requirements: Critical decisions remain anchored in audit and typed failures rather than counters.
Dimensions must stay privacy-safe and correlation-capable.
notes: Metric taxonomy freezes 39 families and COUNTER, GAUGE, HISTOGRAM instruments.
rows.producer_or_owner: Runtime analytics, release governance, and privacy-safe aggregates
rows.retention_boundary: Shorter-lived than audit evidence; no metric row becomes proof of record.
rows.retry_or_resolution_posture: Metrics influence retries and alerts indirectly; they never mutate lineage by themselves.
rows.visibility_boundary: Aggregated operator and release-governance surfaces only.

closure_requirements: Do not reintroduce masked, secret, or authority-sensitive data.
Do not reconstruct dashboard truth from logs alone.
notes: Log families: RUNTIME, SESSION_SECURITY, ACCESS_CONTROL, PRIVACY_RETENTION, AUTHORITY_EDGE.
rows.producer_or_owner: Structured runtime logging with secure access tiers
rows.retention_boundary: Operational logs may rotate quickly and are explicitly shorter-lived than append-only audit evidence.
rows.retry_or_resolution_posture: Log records can evidence runtime context but cannot close or reopen failure lineages.
rows.visibility_boundary: Tiered by access_tier and never a substitute for audit truth.

closure_requirements: Keep exact blocked or mismatch reason codes visible.
Preserve binding refs for step-up, delegation, and export anomalies.
notes: Security telemetry focuses on access decisions, privilege changes, step-up, export attempts, and session anomalies.
rows.producer_or_owner: Access control, session security, and authority-binding anomaly surfaces
rows.query_contract_or_projection: Correlated through get_audit_trail(root_ref, options) and security investigations
rows.retention_boundary: Durable enough for investigation, but still distinct from append-only audit proof.
rows.retry_or_resolution_posture: Security anomalies may open investigations or block retries; they never silently auto-close.
rows.source_heading_or_logical_block: 14.2 Separation of concerns / C. Security telemetry
rows.visibility_boundary: Security-restricted operational surfaces with explicit customer-safe redaction.

closure_requirements: Keep limitation posture explicit when data is minimized or erased.
No erasure may destroy the proof that erasure happened.
notes: Privacy telemetry focuses on masking, legal hold, erasure, and sensitive-view access.
rows.retention_boundary: Retention-limited but explicit; lawful absence must remain distinguishable from corruption.
rows.retry_or_resolution_posture: Privacy issues open typed errors, remediation, or accepted-risk branches rather than quiet omission.
rows.source_heading_or_logical_block: 14.2 Separation of concerns / D. Privacy telemetry and 14.10 Audit versus telemetry retention
rows.visibility_boundary: Privacy-restricted surfaces and dedicated customer-safe explanations only.

closure_requirements: No open error without lawful owner, next path, or scheduled retry.
No accepted-risk refs outside ACCEPTED_RISK state.
notes: Error families: AUTHN_ERROR, AUTHZ_ERROR, MANIFEST_ERROR, CONFIG_ERROR, INPUT_BOUNDARY_ERROR, SOURCE_COLLECTION_ERROR, CANONICALIZATION_ERROR, DATA_QUALITY_ERROR, PARITY_ERROR, TRUST_ERROR, WORKFLOW_ERROR, AUTHORITY_PROTOCOL_ERROR, AUTHORITY_RECONCILIATION_ERROR, AMENDMENT_ERROR, RETENTION_ERROR, PRIVACY_ERROR, PROVENANCE_ERROR, IDEMPOTENCY_ERROR, SYSTEM_FAULT.
Resolution states: OPEN, IN_PROGRESS, MONITORING, RESOLVED, ACCEPTED_RISK, SUPERSEDED, CANCELLED.
rows.retention_boundary: Governed by error retention class and companion artifact_retention_ref when retention/privacy is causal.
rows.retry_or_resolution_posture: Carries severity, blocking class, retry class, remediation class, and resolution state as closed sets.
rows.visibility_boundary: Operator-visible typed failure detail with customer and operator visibility classes.

closure_requirements: Task closure must preserve evidence and effect on the source error.
Task completion cannot silently claim source-error closure.
notes: Task taxonomy includes 12 minimum task types.
rows.producer_or_owner: Tracked remediation and follow-up work boundary
rows.retention_boundary: Persists as a typed lifecycle child object until effect on the source error is closed.
rows.retry_or_resolution_posture: Carries task type, state, priority, and explicit error_resolution_effect.
rows.source_heading_or_logical_block: 13.8 Remediation task object and 13.11 Resolution states
rows.visibility_boundary: Operator and workflow coordination surfaces only.

closure_requirements: Outcome, evidence, and accepted-risk linkage remain durable.
No investigation collapses into a UI-only note.
notes: Investigation classes: AUTHORITY_STATE_AMBIGUITY, AMENDMENT_READINESS, RETENTION_PRIVACY_EXCEPTION, AUDIT_PROVENANCE_DIVERGENCE, SECURITY_ACCESS_ANOMALY, SYSTEM_INVARIANT_BREACH, MULTI_ERROR_CORRELATION.
rows.producer_or_owner: Durable forensic branch for unresolved ambiguity
rows.query_contract_or_projection: Failure lifecycle dashboard projection; get_provenance(object_type, object_id, options)
rows.retention_boundary: Persists through ambiguity, accepted risk, or supersession resolution.
rows.retry_or_resolution_posture: Investigation stays open as a typed branch until a durable outcome exists.

closure_requirements: Retain owner, verification basis, and closure evidence.
Do not hide the underlying error lineage.
notes: Compensation modes: NONE, MARK_AS_VOID, MARK_AS_SUPERSEDED, REVERT_DERIVED_ONLY, OPEN_RECONCILIATION, PRESERVE_AND_LIMIT, REQUIRE_MANUAL_SETTLEMENT.
rows.retention_boundary: Persists until compensation is verified, cancelled, superseded, or archived under policy.
rows.retry_or_resolution_posture: Compensation does not replace the underlying error chain; it alters settlement posture.
rows.visibility_boundary: Operator-visible settlement lineage, never customer-safe by default.

closure_requirements: Approval basis, bounded scope, and expiry are mandatory.
Current accountable owner remains visible while active.
notes: Approval states: ACTIVE, EXPIRED, REVOKED, SUPERSEDED.
Approver types: APPROVER, TENANT_ADMIN, SECURITY_OPERATOR, SYSTEM_POLICY.
rows.retention_boundary: Retains approval lineage, scope, and expiry for the life of the exception.
rows.retry_or_resolution_posture: Accepted risk does not close lineage silently; it creates an explicit risk posture with expiry review.
rows.visibility_boundary: Operator, approver, tenant-admin, or security-operator surfaces depending on scope.

closure_requirements: No log-only or free-text status reconstruction.
Underlying error remains visible through compensation and accepted risk.
notes: Current lineage states: OPEN_FAILURE, RETRY_SCHEDULED, REMEDIATION_ACTIVE, INVESTIGATION_ACTIVE, COMPENSATION_ACTIVE, ACCEPTED_RISK_ACTIVE, RESOLVED, SUPERSEDED, CANCELLED.
Source artifact types: ERROR_RECORD, REMEDIATION_TASK, COMPENSATION_RECORD, FAILURE_INVESTIGATION, ACCEPTED_RISK_APPROVAL, WORKFLOW_ITEM.
Owner types: SYSTEM, SERVICE_OPERATOR, REVIEWER, APPROVER, CLIENT, TENANT_ADMIN, SECURITY_OPERATOR.
rows.retention_boundary: Persists as the read-side truth for one governed lineage until archival policy says otherwise.
rows.retry_or_resolution_posture: Projects current lineage state, owner, next legal action, and compensation/investigation/risk posture from typed objects only.
rows.visibility_boundary: Operator-facing failure inspection and downstream surface projections only.
